answer: An integer value that defines the duration an asset is valid in seconds or one of the following strings: no-store (equivalent to -1), no-cache (equivalent to 0).
If a TTL is not explicitly set for status code 304, we automatically set it to match the TTL of status code 200 (if the user has defined one for 200).

If a user specifies a TTL of one hour for status 200 and 0 seconds (cache and always revalidate) for status 304, the asset will be cached for 1 hour. After it expires, we revalidate with the origin. If the origin returns a 304, each subsequent request will trigger revalidation. If the origin continues to return 304, this cycle will persist.
